The apostle notices a blatant abuse, overlooked by the Corinthians. Party spirit and a false idea of Christian liberty seem to have kept the offender from criticism. It is truly sad that crimes sometimes happen among people who claim to follow the gospel, crimes that even non-believers would be ashamed of. Spiritual pride and false teachings tend to introduce and spread such scandals. How terrible the effects of sin! The devil rules where Christ does not. And a person is in his kingdom and under his power when they are not in Christ. The poor example of an influential person is very harmful; it spreads far and wide. Corrupt principles and examples, if not corrected, would damage the entire church. Believers must have new hearts and live new lives. Their everyday conversations and religious actions must be holy. The sacrifice of Christ as our Passover for us does not make personal and public holiness unnecessary; instead, it provides strong reasons and motives for it. Without holiness we cannot live by faith in him or participate in his ordinances with comfort and benefit.

Christians are to avoid close relationships with all who shame the Christian name. Such people are only suitable companions for their brothers and sisters in sin, and they should be left alone whenever possible. Sadly, there are many people called Christians whose conversations are more dangerous than those of non-believers!
